questions.q: In which year was the film studio Bombay Talkies founded?
questions.explanation: Bombay Talkies was established in 1934 by Himanshu Rai and Devika Rani.

questions.q: Which couple co-founded the Bombay Talkies studio?
questions.explanation: Bombay Talkies was co-founded by the husband-and-wife team of Himanshu Rai and Devika Rani.

questions.q: In which Bombay suburb was the Bombay Talkies studio located?
questions.explanation: The Bombay Talkies studio was set up in Malad, in the northern suburbs of Bombay.

questions.q: The 1936 social reform film Achhut Kanya was produced by which studio?
questions.explanation: Achhut Kanya (1936), addressing caste discrimination, was a landmark production of Bombay Talkies.

questions.q: Which major star of Indian cinema was launched by Bombay Talkies and became famous as a leading man of the 1930s and 40s?
questions.explanation: Ashok Kumar rose from a laboratory assistant at Bombay Talkies to become one of its biggest stars.

questions.q: Bombay Talkies primarily produced films during which era of Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Bombay Talkies was a leading studio of the sound, or talkie, era that began in the 1930s.

questions.q: Which legendary actress, the wife of Himanshu Rai, was a co-founder and leading lady of Bombay Talkies?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani, often called the First Lady of Indian Cinema, was married to Himanshu Rai and co-founded and starred at Bombay Talkies.

questions.q: Bombay Talkies was known for employing technical experts from which country in its early years?
questions.explanation: Bombay Talkies recruited German technicians, who brought advanced filmmaking expertise to the studio.

questions.q: Which female star, who later became a celebrated tragedienne, was introduced to films through Bombay Talkies?
questions.explanation: Madhubala began her film career as a child and young actress associated with Bombay Talkies.

questions.q: Ashok Kumar's frequent leading lady in early Bombay Talkies hits, including Achhut Kanya, was which actress?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani was paired with Ashok Kumar in several Bombay Talkies films, including Achhut Kanya.

questions.q: Who was the German director who directed several early Bombay Talkies films, including Achhut Kanya?
questions.explanation: Franz Osten directed many of the studio's early successes, including Achhut Kanya (1936).

questions.q: Which 1943 Bombay Talkies film starring Ashok Kumar became a massive box-office success and ran for years in a single Calcutta cinema?
questions.explanation: Kismet (1943), produced by Bombay Talkies and starring Ashok Kumar, ran for years at Calcutta's Roxy cinema and was a landmark commercial hit.

questions.q: The 1949 Bombay Talkies film Mahal, famous for the song 'Aayega Aanewala', starred Ashok Kumar opposite which actress?
questions.explanation: Mahal (1949) paired Ashok Kumar with Madhubala and is considered an early Hindi suspense classic.

questions.q: The haunting song 'Aayega Aanewala' from the Bombay Talkies film Mahal launched the playback career of which singer?
questions.explanation: 'Aayega Aanewala' in Mahal (1949) brought Lata Mangeshkar her first major breakthrough as a playback singer.

questions.q: Which famous tragedian, later known as the 'Tragedy King', was launched by Bombay Talkies in the early 1940s?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar made his film debut with Bombay Talkies in Jwar Bhata (1944).

questions.q: What was distinctive about the staffing system that Bombay Talkies operated under?
options: It ran on a professional salaried-staff studio model
It paid actors only per film with no contracts
questions.explanation: Bombay Talkies followed a professional studio system with salaried, contracted staff rather than per-film hires.

questions.q: Devika Rani and Himanshu Rai had earlier worked together on which acclaimed 1933 bilingual film, made before founding Bombay Talkies?
questions.explanation: Karma (1933), made in English and Hindi, starred Devika Rani and preceded the founding of Bombay Talkies.

questions.q: Following the death of Himanshu Rai in 1940, control of Bombay Talkies passed largely to which producer who later split off to form Filmistan?
questions.explanation: After Himanshu Rai's death, internal disputes led Sashadhar Mukherjee and associates to leave and found Filmistan.

questions.q: In which year did Himanshu Rai, the co-founder of Bombay Talkies, die, triggering management turmoil at the studio?
questions.explanation: Himanshu Rai died in 1940, after which Devika Rani and others struggled to keep control of the studio.

questions.q: Achhut Kanya, the famous Bombay Talkies social drama, dealt primarily with which theme?
options: The freedom struggle against the British
questions.explanation: Achhut Kanya told the story of love across caste lines and condemned the practice of untouchability.

questions.q: Before entering Indian cinema, Himanshu Rai gained filmmaking experience by co-producing silent films in collaboration with studios from which country?
questions.explanation: Himanshu Rai produced Indo-German silent films such as The Light of Asia, building ties with German studios.

questions.q: What was the title of the 1925 Indo-German silent film, based on the life of the Buddha, that Himanshu Rai produced and starred in before Bombay Talkies?
questions.explanation: The Light of Asia (Prem Sanyas, 1925) was an early Indo-German co-production starring Himanshu Rai.

questions.q: Which German cinematographer, known for his work at Bombay Talkies, helped train Indian technicians at the studio?
questions.explanation: Josef Wirsching was the German cinematographer central to Bombay Talkies' technical reputation.

questions.q: Ashok Kumar's real name, before he was renamed at Bombay Talkies, was what?
questions.explanation: Ashok Kumar was born Kumudlal Ganguly and adopted his screen name during his Bombay Talkies years.

questions.q: During the Second World War, several German staff at Bombay Talkies, including director Franz Osten, were affected in what way?
options: They were promoted to lead the studio
They were interned as enemy aliens by the British
questions.explanation: When the war broke out, German nationals at Bombay Talkies were interned by the British colonial authorities.

questions.q: Which 1936 Bombay Talkies film paired Ashok Kumar and Devika Rani and is remembered for the song 'Main Ban Ki Chidiya'?
questions.explanation: Achhut Kanya (1936) featured the celebrated duet 'Main Ban Ki Chidiya' sung by its leads, Ashok Kumar and Devika Rani.

questions.q: After the major 1943 walkout, the production unit that left Bombay Talkies established which rival studio?
questions.explanation: The breakaway faction led by Sashadhar Mukherjee founded Filmistan in 1943 as a rival to Bombay Talkies.

questions.q: The first film Devika Rani and Himanshu Rai produced under the Bombay Talkies banner, released in 1935, was titled what?
questions.explanation: Jawani Ki Hawa (1935) was the first film produced by the newly founded Bombay Talkies.

questions.q: By approximately which year had Bombay Talkies ceased operations as a working studio?
questions.explanation: Beset by financial difficulties and departures, Bombay Talkies wound down and closed in the early-to-mid 1950s, its last release being Baadbaan (1954).

questions.q: Which music director, who scored several Bombay Talkies films, composed the music for the studio's hit Kismet (1943)?
questions.explanation: Anil Biswas composed the music for Kismet (1943) and other Bombay Talkies productions.
